mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate decline observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 52% accuracy over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of historical cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflect contracting manufacturing output, which suppresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 2.0% confirms subdued inflation expectations, prompting market participants to price in prolonged accommodative policy and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL falls below 2.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness that erodes consumer spending forecasts, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint divergence condition fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns with > 55% accuracy, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion that supports corporate revenue trajectories, generating consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity rebuilding that boosts equity risk premiums, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 57% of cases, or the mechanism loses predictive power when volatility indices exceed 25.
